response: At the Drive-In and Muse share the commonality of being English rock bands. Muse, formed in 1994, consists of Matt Bellamy (lead vocals, guitar, piano, keyboards), Chris Wolstenholme (bass guitar, backing vocals, keyboards), and Dominic Howard (drums, percussion). At the Drive-In, formed in 1994, is an American punk rock band from El Paso, Texas, consisting of Cedric Bixler (vocals), Omar Rodríguez (guitar, vocals), Paul Hinojos (bass), Tony Hajjar (drums), and Keeley Davis (guitar, vocals). Both bands have contributed to the rock music scene, with Muse known for their progressive rock and At the Drive-In for their post-hardcore and punk influences.
